canonical_question: How do I harden database security?
aliases: PostgreSQL MySQL MongoDB security best practices
how to protect database from attacks

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: PostgreSQL 16 default to scram-sha-256 (2023); MySQL 8.0 default to caching_sha2_password (2018); MongoDB 6.0 required authentication by default (2022)

constraints: NEVER run database services as root/Administrator -- always use a dedicated low-privilege OS account
NEVER allow remote connections without TLS/SSL encryption in production
NEVER use default credentials (postgres/postgres, root with no password, admin/admin) in any environment
NEVER grant superuser or DBA privileges to application service accounts
ALWAYS enforce authentication for ALL connections including localhost
Backup encryption keys MUST be stored separately from backup media

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need application-level SQL injection prevention, not database-level hardening
Need web application firewall or WAF configuration
Need cloud-managed database security (RDS, Cloud SQL, Azure SQL)

inputs_needed.question: Which database engine are you using?
